overview: Kaleidoscope currently treats every audit, screenshot, and report as an ephemeral one-off. There is no way to track how a project's interface changes over time, detect unintentional regressions, or compare state before and after a code change. This feature introduces a project config, snapshot system, diff engine, baseline manager, HTML diff report, and pre-commit hook integration — giving AI agents and developers a complete visual regression detection workflow.
goals: Enable chronological tracking of interface state across commits
Detect audit regressions (new contrast violations, increased issue counts) automatically
Detect element-level DOM changes (appeared, disappeared, moved, resized) via accessibility tree comparison
Detect pixel-level visual differences between screenshots
Provide structured JSON output for programmatic agent consumption and HTML reports for visual review
Integrate into git pre-commit hooks for automatic regression checks
nonGoals: CI/CD pipeline integration beyond pre-commit hooks
Multi-branch tracking (snapshots are linear, not branch-aware)
Visual regression testing against design mockups (Figma, Sketch)
Automatic remediation (the agent decides; kaleidoscope only reports)
successMetrics: AI agent can detect and report regressions in a pre-commit hook without manual invocation
Diff engine correctly identifies new vs resolved audit issues per category
Element comparison via accessibility tree matches on semantic identity (role + name)
Pixel diff produces visual highlight overlays with configurable similarity threshold
openQuestions: Should snapshot storage deduplicate identical screenshots across runs to save space?
Should `ks diff` support comparing two arbitrary snapshots (not just latest vs baseline)?
rules: All new commands follow the existing `output.Result` JSON convention via `output.Success`/`output.Fail
Snapshot commands reuse existing audit, screenshot, and ax-tree logic internally (no shelling out to the CLI)
Screenshot diffing must be pure Go using standard `image` and `image/png` packages (no ImageMagick)
.ks-project.json` is committed to the repo; `.kaleidoscope/snapshots/` is gitignored
.kaleidoscope/baselines.json` is committed to the repo (shared baseline)
The diff engine is a pure-function module (two data structures in, diff out) with no Chrome dependency

stories.title: Project Config Commands
stories.description:
As a developer, I want to define a kaleidoscope project as a set of URL paths to track, so that snapshot and diff commands know which pages to monitor.

Introduces `.ks-project.json` and four commands: `ks init --name <name> --base-url <url> --paths /,/dashboard`, `ks project-add <path>`, `ks project-remove <path>`, `ks project-show`. Breakpoints default to the four standard presets (375x812, 768x1024, 1280x720, 1920x1080).
acceptanceCriteria: ks init --name <name> --base-url <url> --paths /,/dashboard` creates a valid `.ks-project.json` in the current directory
ks init` returns an error if `.ks-project.json` already exists
ks project-add /settings` appends the path and writes updated config to disk
ks project-remove /settings` removes the path and writes updated config to disk
ks project-show` outputs the full project config as structured JSON via `output.Result
Breakpoints default to all four standard presets when not specified
Attempting to add a duplicate path returns an error
Attempting to remove a non-existent path returns an error

stories.title: Snapshot Capture and History
stories.description:
As an AI agent, I want to automatically capture full interface state for every URL in a project, so that I can compare state before and after code changes.

Introduces `ks snapshot` (iterates project URLs, captures screenshots at all breakpoints, runs audit, dumps ax-tree, persists under `.kaleidoscope/snapshots/<timestamp>-<short-commit-hash>/`) and `ks history` (lists snapshots in reverse chronological order). On first snapshot with no baseline, auto-promotes to baseline in `.kaleidoscope/baselines.json`.
acceptanceCriteria: ks snapshot` creates a correctly structured directory under `.kaleidoscope/snapshots/` with one subdirectory per URL path
Each URL subdirectory contains 4 breakpoint PNGs, `audit.json`, and `ax-tree.json
Root `snapshot.json` manifest includes timestamp, commit hash (when in a git repo), and project config at capture time
Snapshot ID format is `<timestamp>-<short-commit-hash>` or timestamp-only outside git
First snapshot with no existing baseline auto-creates `.kaleidoscope/baselines.json
ks history` lists snapshots with timestamp, commit hash, and summary stats
ks snapshot` fails gracefully with a clear error if project URLs are unreachable

stories.title: Audit and Element Diff Engine
stories.description:
As an AI agent, I want to compare a snapshot against a known-good baseline and receive structured JSON describing what changed, so that I can distinguish intentional changes from regressions.

Introduces `ks diff [snapshot-id]` which compares a snapshot (default: latest) against the baseline. Reports audit deltas (per-category issue count changes, per-issue new/resolved status by selector matching) and element changes (appeared, disappeared, moved, resized via accessibility tree comparison using semantic identity). Exit code 0 when no regressions, exit code 1 when regressions detected.
acceptanceCriteria: ks diff` compares latest snapshot against baseline and outputs structured JSON
ks diff <snapshot-id>` compares a specific snapshot against baseline
Audit deltas report per-category count changes (contrast, spacing, typography, touch targets)
Audit deltas track per-issue new/resolved status by matching on selector
Element changes detect appeared, disappeared, moved (position delta beyond threshold), and resized elements via ax-tree comparison
Element matching uses semantic identity (role + name) not CSS selectors
Exit code 0 when no regressions detected, exit code 1 when regressions exist
ks diff` returns an error if no baseline exists

stories.title: Screenshot Pixel Diff
stories.description:
As a developer, I want pixel-level visual comparison between baseline and current screenshots that produces a highlight overlay image, so that subtle visual changes are surfaced.

Implements pure Go image comparison using standard `image` and `image/png` packages. Produces a diff PNG with highlighted difference regions and a similarity score (0.0-1.0). Integrates into `ks diff` output alongside audit/element diffs.
acceptanceCriteria: Given two PNGs of the same dimensions, produces a diff PNG highlighting changed pixels
Returns a similarity score (0.0-1.0) for each screenshot pair
Diff images are written to the snapshot directory alongside source screenshots
ks diff` JSON output includes screenshot diff results (paths to diff images, similarity scores per URL per breakpoint)
Handles mismatched image dimensions gracefully by reporting as full regression rather than panicking
Configurable similarity threshold controls whether a screenshot pair is flagged as regressed

stories.title: Baseline Manager
stories.description:
As an AI agent, I want to accept a snapshot as the new baseline with `ks accept`, so that intentional changes do not trigger false regression alerts in future diff runs.

Introduces `ks accept [snapshot-id]` which promotes a snapshot (default: latest) to baseline for all project URLs by updating `.kaleidoscope/baselines.json`. Supports `--url <path>` to accept for a single URL only.
acceptanceCriteria: ks accept` promotes the latest snapshot to baseline for all project URLs
ks accept <snapshot-id>` promotes a specific snapshot
ks accept --url /dashboard` updates baseline for only that URL path, leaving others unchanged
.kaleidoscope/baselines.json` is correctly updated on disk after accept
ks accept` returns an error if no snapshots exist
Accepting a snapshot that is already the baseline is a no-op (idempotent)

stories.title: Side-by-Side HTML Diff Report
stories.description:
As a developer, I want a visual HTML report showing baseline vs current screenshots side-by-side with diff overlays and audit delta tables, so that I can quickly review what changed.

Introduces `ks diff-report [snapshot-id] [--output path]`. Extends the existing `report/` HTML template engine. Layout: baseline (left), current (right), pixel diff overlay (center) for each URL and breakpoint. Below each section: audit delta tables and element change lists. Self-contained HTML with base64-embedded screenshots.
acceptanceCriteria: ks diff-report` generates a self-contained HTML file comparing latest snapshot vs baseline
Side-by-side layout shows baseline (left), current (right), and diff overlay (center) per URL per breakpoint
Audit delta tables show per-category before/after/delta counts below each URL section
Element change lists show each change with selector, type (appeared/disappeared/moved/resized), and details
--output` flag controls output path; defaults to `.kaleidoscope/diff-report.html
Screenshots are base64-embedded (matching existing `ks report` approach)
Report fails gracefully with clear error if no baseline or no snapshots exist

stories.title: Pre-Commit Hook Integration
stories.description:
As an AI agent, I want a pre-commit hook that automatically runs snapshot and diff on every commit, so that regression checks happen without manual invocation.

Introduces `ks install-hook` which writes a git pre-commit hook to `.git/hooks/pre-commit`. The hook runs `ks snapshot && ks diff`, outputs diff JSON to stdout, always exits 0 (advisory/non-blocking), auto-starts Chrome if not running, and fails gracefully if URLs are unreachable.
acceptanceCriteria: ks install-hook` writes an executable script to `.git/hooks/pre-commit
The hook runs `ks snapshot` followed by `ks diff` on commit
Hook outputs structured diff JSON to stdout for agent consumption
Hook always exits 0 (advisory, non-blocking) so the agent retains control
Hook auto-starts Chrome via `ks start` if not already running
Hook fails gracefully with a clear message if project URLs are unreachable
ks install-hook` warns if a pre-commit hook already exists and does not silently overwrite
Running `ks install-hook` when no `.ks-project.json` exists returns an error
